Third clustering column; higher cardinality than status aids pruning for job-role-specific analytical queries; source NOT NULL constraint verified post-load
BigQuery NUMERIC supports 29 digits of precision and 9 decimal places, fully accommodating decimal(8,2); no precision loss; source NOT NULL constraint verified post-load
Nullable in source; BigQuery NUMERIC safely accommodates decimal(4,2); null rate should be reported in data quality output
Nullable; scale is zero so lossless cast to INT64; self-referential FK to employee_id; referential integrity validated as part of post-load data quality checks
First and highest-priority clustering column; scale is zero so INT64 cast is lossless; nullable in source — null rows will appear in clustering block boundary but do not break partitioning

Second clustering column; low-cardinality values (e.g. ACTIVE, INACTIVE) maximise clustering efficiency for status-filter queries common in operational reporting; source NOT NULL constraint verified post-load
MySQL TIMESTAMP is stored and retrieved in UTC by the Cloud SQL connector; maps directly to BigQuery TIMESTAMP; NOT NULL in source verified post-load

Partition column with DAY granularity; configure partition_expiration_ms = 5184000000 (60 days) on the BigQuery table to enforce the 60-day retention SLA; also the second component of the composite merge key (employee_id, updated_date) used by sp_mysqltobq_load.sql; rows with null updated_date route to the __NULL__ partition which is exempt from partition expiry — document and handle in retention strategy
